narrative:
ALONE AGAINST THE DARK - Point Black Lighthouse

    You arrive at the remote Point Black Lighthouse on the rocky Maine coast.
    Lieutenant Warner called you: the lighthouse keeper has vanished.
    But when you examine the logs, something is wrong. The keeper was dead for two weeks
    before anyone found him—yet someone has been writing in the logs ever since.

    The fog rolls in. The light blinks red. Something ancient stirs beneath the waves.
Player: Analyze psychological impact of events on locals

DM: You approach Warner, who seems nervous but resilient after the series of strange incidents at the lighthouse. He shares his concerns about the mental state of the crew, which has been increasingly unstable since the unexplained disappearance of their keeper. Despite this, they are trying to keep up with their duties and maintain the beacon's vigilance.

As you try to persuade Warner into revealing more details about the crew, your psychology skill kicks in. Rolling a d100, your attempt at understanding emotions succeeds: 73 (Success)

You perceive his worries, but also sense his dedication and need to keep their community safe. He opens up, confessing that many of them have been hearing strange noises coming from beneath the waves, making them nervous. Some claim they've seen phantom shapes in the water - eerie shadows that vanish before reaching land.

As a psychiatrist, you suggest they could be suffering from post-traumatic stress or mass hysteria, exacerbated by their fear of the dark sea monsters. Warner agrees to consider seeking professional help for his crew and urges you to investigate these sea phenomena further. You begin your investigation into these underwater terrors:
Player: Interview witnesses about mental state

DM: Dr. Amelia Cross, a psychologist, investigates the mental impact of recent strange events at Point Black Lighthouse after Warner shares his concerns regarding the crew's unstable psychological state. As Dr. Cross listens to their experiences and symptoms, it becomes apparent that many may be suffering from post-traumatic stress or mass hysteria due to fearful accounts of sea monsters. With this knowledge in mind, Dr. Cross plans to continue her investigation into the terrifying underwater creatures while working on a potential mental health solution for the community.
Player: Search for signs of psychological breakdown

DM: As you investigate the lighthouse, hoping to locate clues about the cause of the crew's disturbing behavior, your eyes fall on an old logbook from previous expeditions. The pages are filled with strange notes and unexplained occurrences - sightings of ghostly beings, eerie shadows in the water, and otherworldly phenomena that seem unrelated to sea creatures.


Player: Look for answers in logbook entries
DM: As you try to decipher the cryptic scribbles, a faint noise draws your attention back to the present. You glance at the water outside, only to see a dark shadowy form rising from the depths. The deep one hybrid appears, hungry for more blood - it is time to fight! 

You quickly grab a nearby weapon and prepare to defend yourself. As the beast emerges from the waves, its predatory gaze fixes on you with unwavering hunger. The battle is joined...

Player: Attempt to dodge the deep one's attack
DM: As the Deep One hybrid lunges at you, your body twists and turns in a desperate attempt to avoid the savage blow. Unfortunately, your reflexes are not quick enough. The creature connects, ripping into your side with its razor-sharp claws. 

You feel warm blood pour from the wound, but you must press on and fight back against the dark creature threatening your life. You raise your weapon and prepare to face it once again...

DM: Player Action - Investigate Keeper's Mental History
Dr. Amelia Cross enters the keeper's quarters, carefully examining each room and item to discover any clues about the previous keeper's state of mind. The environment is a mix of chaos and serenity, revealing that the former keeper led an unusual yet orderly life. A few unsettling notes seem out of place, referring to disturbing underwater creatures and otherworldly entities.

You can feel your heartbeat quicken as you continue searching for answers. An old journal, hidden beneath a pile of crates, catches your eye. Carefully flipping through the pages, you find a series of strange drawings depicting an array of unknown sea monsters and otherworldly beings. As you study these images, you can't help but feel unnerved by their disturbing nature. The truth behind the events at Point Black Lighthouse seems to be closer than ever.

As you prepare your next action, there is no roll request necessary since you have already succeeded in your investigation. You may now consider exploring further or examining a specific location within the lighthouse. However, if you were unsuccessful in your attempt and failed in your investigation, it might lead to encountering new horrors or challenges as your character's psyche is pushed beyond its limits.

DM: Player Action - Confront Underlying Psychological Horror
As you continue through the lighthouse, Dr. Cross begins to experience feelings of fear and unease. Amidst the chaos and clues pointing toward dark creatures lurking in the water, she struggles to separate reality from her own imagination. Your mind is plagued by vivid nightmares that only serve to intensify her fears about what truly happened here.

Despite these feelings of dread, you push forward. As you delve deeper into your investigation, you find further evidence of the previous keeper's mental state deteriorating over time. With each new discovery, it becomes more apparent that their world was twisted by a combination of stress, trauma, and mysterious forces beyond human comprehension.

While the reality of what happened at Point Black Lighthouse remains uncertain, one thing is certain: Your character will face immense mental challenges as they work to uncover the truth behind the psychological horror plaguing both themselves and their community. To do this, you may consider seeking help from others or turning towards your own inner strength. In either case, prepare yourself for a battle against not just creatures but also the unknown terrors that lurk within one's psyche.

DM: As you continue your investigation into the Keeper's mental state and the underlying horrors they may have faced, you attempt to document every clue and piece of evidence gathered so far. While cataloging your findings, you notice a recurring theme in your notes: the presence of unknown underwater creatures with unnatural powers.

You feel a growing sense of dread as you realize these creatures might hold the key to understanding what happened at Point Black Lighthouse. As your investigation progresses, be prepared for new challenges and encounters that may test both your sanity and physical resilience. The struggle continues, with even more unnerving discoveries just beyond your grasp...
recent_actions: Search for signs of psychological breakdown

lighthouse_exterior.name: Point Black Lighthouse - Exterior
lighthouse_exterior.base_description: A weathered lighthouse stands on rocky black stone, its paint peeling from decades of exposure to salt spray and Atlantic winds. The structure is perhaps thirty feet tall, surrounded by a low stone wall. The keeper's cottage sits nearby, its windows dark and empty.

keeper_quarters.name: Keeper's Quarters
keeper_quarters.base_description: The keeper's sparse living space: a cot, a table with a single chair, shelves of maritime equipment. Everything is covered in dust. Through the single window, you can see the lighthouse. There's a faint smell of decay and something else—something chemical.

lighthouse_interior.name: Lighthouse Interior
lighthouse_interior.base_description: The interior of the lighthouse is a spiral of iron stairs ascending into darkness. The air is thick and stale. Each step groans under your weight. The walls are covered in moisture and a strange luminescent fungus that glows faintly green in the darkness.

keeper_room_top.name: Keeper's Room - Lighthouse Top
keeper_room_top.base_description: At the top of the lighthouse, a small room contains a cot, desk, and the great lamp mechanism. Papers are scattered everywhere—logbooks, journals, sketches of impossible symbols. The light mechanism hasn't been lit in weeks. Through the windows, you can see the entire rocky coast and far out to sea.
